title: The Rangers' Empty House
description: Eldertree's four rangers have not returned from their patrol. They are never late. Their house at the top of the path is empty, and someone came back afterward to tidy it.

offer.speaker: Elder Vesper
offer.text: The rangers are late. They are never late. They know the trees better than the trees know themselves. So: not lost.

offer.speaker: Elder Vesper
offer.text: I will not say what I think has happened, because saying it invites it to be true. I will ask you to go look.

accept.speaker: Elder Vesper
accept.text: Their house is at the top of the path. Look through it. Look for what's NOT there. That's where they'll be.

accept.speaker: Elder Vesper
accept.text: If the woods talk while you're in them, walk quicker. If the woods stop talking, walk quicker still.

decline.speaker: Elder Vesper
decline.text: You have your reasons. So do the woods. The rangers used to have theirs, too.

in_progress.speaker: Elder Vesper
in_progress.text: Any sign? The forest is quiet. Not empty — quiet. Something is being quiet through it.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Elder Vesper
ready_to_turn_in.text: You came back. The woods let you. That is a thing worth noticing.

complete.speaker: Elder Vesper
complete.text: You met the hunter. Then he is not our rangers' hunter. Our rangers were his prey. That is a distinction that only matters if you count the difference between hunted and gone.

complete.speaker: Elder Vesper
complete.text: The house you found tidied — that wasn't the rangers. That was someone who wanted us to notice they were tidying. That's a message. I'll pretend I haven't received it, for now.

complete.speaker: Elder Vesper
complete.text: Take one of their cloaks. There are three left, and none of them belong to anyone anymore.

objectives.description: Hear Elder Vesper's request in Eldertree.
Search the rangers' empty house at the top of the village path.
Enter the forest. Find what is hunting there.

rogue.description: Rogue examine text at the rangers' house: the tidying is TOO careful. Every arrow in the quiver is in the same rotation. Rangers never store arrows like that — the pattern is administrative, not martial. 'Someone who has never held a bow arranged these.' No mechanical effect.

cross_world_connection: The 'tidied' pattern at the ranger house — every object placed at the exact same angle — reappears in W2 as the HOA's compliance-inspection markers. The person who tidied the rangers' house was practicing.
_wiring_notes: Step 2 custom flag emitted by an examine tile inside the rangers' house (cowir-overworld places the tile; text hook = 'Someone came back after and tidied.'). Step 3 shared flag w1_tempo_defeated wired to the Tempo Masterite encounter (Lane B). If Tempo not placed, quest sits at step 3. Design doc: docs/design/w1-progression-expansion.md, Lane C row 2. Item rangers_cloak = new item, will need to be added to items.json (defer to items-lane or overworld's fold — flavor: 'Cloak of a ranger who did not come home. Warm. Not comforting.').
